nodes.context: Street gang from Croxteth, Liverpool, involved in feud with Strand Gang.
Street gang from Norris Green, Liverpool, rival of Crocky Crew.
Secretive drug cartel from Huyton, Liverpool, founded in 1990s, internationally active.
Major drug trafficking organization led by Curtis Warren, linked to Cali Cartel.
Colombian cocaine cartel that supplied drugs to Liverpool gangs.
First drug cartel to develop in the UK, active in Liverpool in the 1980s.
Family gang from Anfield, Liverpool, involved in drug smuggling.
Family-run international drug empire based in Liverpool, jailed in 2013.
Liverpool drugs gang led by Peter and Stephen Clarke, shipped drugs to Northern Ireland.
Criminal firm led by John Haase and Paul Bennett, involved in bank robbery and drug smuggling.
Drug gang from Speke, Liverpool, targeted by Huyton Firm in Amsterdam assassination plot.

nodes.original_text_name: local gang led by Kirk Bradley and Tony Downes
nodes.context: Gang hired by Huyton Firm to attack people associated with a trial in 2009.

edges.context: Feud between Crocky Crew and Strand Gang led to multiple shootings and murders, including Rhys Jones.
Curtis Warren forged direct links with the Cali Cartel to flood Europe with drugs.
Liverpool Mafia developed distribution networks with cocaine producers in South America, including the Cali cartel.
The Huyton Firm filled the power vacuum left after Curtis Warren and his gang were jailed.
Huyton Firm dispatched a hit team to Amsterdam to assassinate a member of the Speke-based drug gang.
Huyton Firm paid Kirk Bradley's gang to attack people associated with a trial in 2009.
